action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|##..A.#..|
 2|.p#.#....|
 3|1..##...y|
 4|x#...#...|
 5|.#S.##q..|
 6|..#..#...|
 7|.....2..#|
 8|....#....|
 9|.#..B.#..|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (3,5) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (3,5), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (5,1): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {0, 1}. So it is open at t=0,1,4,5,... and closed at t=2,3,6,7,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (5,9): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (2,2); t mod 8=1: (1,2); t mod 8=2: (1,3); t mod 8=3: (2,3); t mod 8=4: (3,3); t mod 8=5: (2,3); t mod 8=6: (1,3); t mod 8=7: (1,2). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (7,5); t mod 6=1: (7,6); t mod 6=2: (7,7); t mod 6=3: (6,7); t mod 6=4: (7,7); t mod 6=5: (7,6). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (1,3) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (1,4) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (6,7) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (9,3) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 40 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 8 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive only a per-tick log of OK/BUMP/CAUGHT/PICKED UP/DELIVERED. You are NEVER told where you are or why you bumped - track your position yourself.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I need to pick up and deliver two parcels. Parcel 1 is at (1,3) and needs to go to (1,4). Parcel 2 is at (6,7) and needs to go to (9,3). I start at (3,5) at t=0.

Let me analyze the schedules:
- Gate A at (5,1): open at t mod 4 ∈ {0,1}, so open at t=0,1,4,5,8,9,...
- Gate B at (5,9): open at t mod 2 = 1, so open at t=1,3,5,7,9,...
- Guard P cycle (8 ticks): (2,2),(1,2),(1,3),(2,3),(3,3),(2,3),(1,3),(1,2)
- Guard Q cycle (6 ticks): (7,5),(7,6),(7,7),(6,7),(7,7),(7,6)

I should pick up parcel 1 first since it's closer and the delivery location (1,4) is also nearby.

At t=0, I'm at (3,5). Guard P is at (2,2), Guard Q is at (7,5).

Let me plan a route to (1,3):
- t=0: at (3,5)
- t=1: move W to (2,5) - Guard P at (1,2), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe
- t=2: move W to (1,5) - Guard P at (1,3), Guard Q at (7,7) - safe (P is at (1,3), I'm at (1,5))
- t=3: move N to (1,4) - Guard P at (2,3), Guard Q at (6,7) - safe
- t=4: move N to (1,3) - Guard P at (3,3), Guard Q at (7,7) - safe, PICK UP parcel 1
- t=5: move S to (1,4) - Guard P at (2,3), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe, DELIVER parcel 1

So after 5 ticks, I'll be at (1,4) with parcel 1 delivered. Then I need to go to (6,7) to pick up parcel 2.

From (1,4), I need to get to (6,7). Let me continue:
- t=5: at (1,4) - parcel 1 delivered
- t=6: move E to (2,4) - Guard P at (1,3), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe
- t=7: move E to (3,4) - Guard P at (1,2), Guard Q at (7,5) - safe
- t=8: move E to (4,4) - Guard P at (2,2), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe
- t=9: move E to (5,4) - Guard P at (1,3), Guard Q at (6,7) - safe

So after 9 ticks total, I'll be at (5,4). Then I need to continue to (6,7).

From (5,4), I need to go to (6,7):
- t=10: move S to (5,5) - Guard P at (2,3), Guard Q at (7,7) - safe
- t=11: move S to (5,6) - Guard P at (3,3), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe
- t=12: move S to (5,7) - Guard P at (2,3), Guard Q at (7,5) - safe
- t=13: move E to (6,7) - Guard P at (1,2), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe, PICK UP parcel 2

So after 13 ticks, I'll have both parcels and be at (6,7). Now I need to deliver parcel 2 to (9,3).

From (6,7) to (9,3):
- t=14: move E to (7,7) - Guard P at (1,3), Guard Q at (6,7) - safe
- t=15: move E to (8,7) - Guard P at (2,2), Guard Q at (7,7) - CAUGHT! Guard Q will be at (7,7) at t=15.

I need to avoid (7,7) at t=15. Let me reconsider:
- t=14: move E to (7,7) - Guard Q at (6,7) - safe
- t=15: move N to (7,6) - Guard P at (1,2), Guard Q at (7,7) - safe (Q is at (7,7), I'm at (7,6))
- t=16: move N to (7,5) - Guard P at (1,3), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe (Q is at (7,6), I'm at (7,5))
- t=17: move N to (7,4) - Guard P at (2,3), Guard Q at (7,7) - safe
- t=18: move N to (7,3) - Guard P at (3,3), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe

From (7,3) to (9,3):
- t=19: move E to (8,3) - Guard P at (2,3), Guard Q at (7,5) - safe
- t=20: move E to (9,3) - Guard P at (1,2), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe, DELIVER parcel 2

This would complete everything in 20 ticks, well under the 40 tick limit.

Let me verify the first 5 moves more carefully:
- t=0: at (3,5)
- t=1: W to (2,5) - OK
- t=2: W to (1,5) - OK (P at (1,3), Q at (7,7))
- t=3: N to (1,4) - OK (P at (2,3), Q at (6,7))
- t=4: N to (1,3) - OK (P at (3,3), Q at (7,7)), PICK UP parcel 1
- t=5: S to (1,4) - OK (P at (2,3), Q at (7,6)), DELIVER parcel 1

After 5 ticks, I'm at (1,4) with parcel 1 delivered. My first plan should get me to (1,4).

POSITION: (1,4)
PLAN: W W N N S
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 1 RESULT (ticks 0 -> 5):
t=1: W -> BUMP.
t=2: W -> BUMP.
t=3: N -> OK.
t=4: N -> CAUGHT! Returned to start.
Capture penalty: the remaining 1 move(s) of your plan were cancelled and the round's leftover ticks were forfeited; the clock jumped to t=5.

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I was caught at t=4 when I moved to (1,3). At t=4, Guard P was at (3,3) according to my schedule, but I was caught. Let me recalculate the guard positions.

Guard P cycle: t mod 8=0: (2,2); t mod 8=1: (1,2); t mod 8=2: (1,3); t mod 8=3: (2,3); t mod 8=4: (3,3); t mod 8=5: (2,3); t mod 8=6: (1,3); t mod 8=7: (1,2)

So at t=4, Guard P is at (3,3), not (1,3). But I was caught at (1,3) at t=4. That means I must have been caught by Guard Q?

Guard Q cycle: t mod 6=0: (7,5); t mod 6=1: (7,6); t mod 6=2: (7,7); t mod 6=3: (6,7); t mod 6=4: (7,7); t mod 6=5: (7,6)

At t=4, Guard Q is at (7,7), not (1,3). So I wasn't caught by either guard at (1,3) at t=4... unless my position tracking was wrong.

Let me reconsider the first 5 moves:
- t=0: at (3,5)
- t=1: W to (2,5) - I got BUMP, so I stayed at (3,5)
- t=2: W to (2,5) - I got BUMP again, so I stayed at (3,5)
- t=3: N to (3,4) - OK, so I'm at (3,4)
- t=4: N to (3,3) - I was CAUGHT! So at t=4, a guard was at (3,3).

At t=4, Guard P is at (3,3) according to the schedule. That's why I was caught!

So after being caught at t=4, I was returned to start (3,5) and the remaining moves were cancelled. The clock jumped to t=5.

So now at t=5, I'm at (3,5). I need to pick up parcel 1 at (1,3) and deliver to (1,4), then pick up parcel 2 at (6,7) and deliver to (9,3).

At t=5:
- Guard P: t mod 8 = 5, so at (2,3)
- Guard Q: t mod 6 = 5, so at (7,6)

Let me plan a new route to (1,3) avoiding the guards:
- t=5: at (3,5)
- t=6: move W to (2,5) - Guard P at (1,3), Guard Q at (7,7) - safe (P is at (1,3), I'm at (2,5))
- t=7: move W to (1,5) - Guard P at (1,2), Guard Q at (7,6) - safe
- t=8: move N to (1,4) - Guard P at (2,2), Guard Q at (7,7) - safe
- t=9: move N to (1,3) - Guard P at (1,3) - CAUGHT! Guard P is at (1,3) at t=9 (t mod 8 = 1).

I need to avoid (1,3) at t=9. Let me try a different approach:
- t=5: at (3,5)
- t=6: move W to (2,5) - safe
- t=7: move W to (1,5) - safe
- t=8: move E to (2,5) - safe (going back)
- t=9: move W to (1,5) - safe again

That's wasteful. Let me try:
- t=5: at (3,5)
- t=6: move W to (2,5) - safe
- t=7: move W to (1,5) - safe
- t=8: move N to (1,4) - safe
- t=9: move E to (2,4) - safe (avoiding (1,3) where P is)

Then I need to get to (1,3) when P is not there. Guard P is at (1,3) when t mod 8 = 2 or 6. So t=2,6,10,14,18,...

At t=10, Guard P is at (2,2) (t mod 8 = 2... wait, t=10 mod 8 = 2, so P is at (1,3)). At t=11, P is at (2,2) (t mod 8 = 3). At t=12, P is at (2,3) (t mod 8 = 4). At t=13, P is at (3,3) (t mod 8 = 5). At t=14, P is at (2,3) (t mod 8 = 6... wait, t=14 mod 8 = 6, so P is at (1,3)). At t=15, P is at (1,2) (t mod 8 = 7). At t=16, P is at (2,2) (t mod 8 = 0).

So P is NOT at (1,3) at t=11, 12, 13, 15, 16...

Let me try:
- t=5: at (3,5)
- t=6: move W to (2,5) - safe
- t=7: move W to (1,5) - safe
- t=8: move N to (1,4) - safe
- t=9: move E to (2,4) - safe (P at (1,3))

Then I need to wait until P leaves (1,3). At t=10, P is at (1,3). At t=11, P is at (2,2). So at t=11, I can move to (1,3).

- t=10: move W to (1,4) - safe (P at (1,3), I'm at (1,4))
- t=11: move N to (1,3) - safe (P at (2,2)), PICK UP parcel 1
- t=12: move S to (1,4) - safe (P at (2,3)), DELIVER parcel 1

So after 7 ticks (t=5 to t=12), I'll be at (1,4) with parcel 1 delivered. But my plan is only 5 moves, so I need to plan for t=5 to t=10 first.

Let me plan 5 moves from t=5:
- t=5: at (3,5)
- t=6: W to (2,5) - safe
- t=7: W to (1,5) - safe
- t=8: N to (1,4) - safe
- t=9: E to (2,4) - safe

After 5 moves, I'll be at (2,4) at t=10.

POSITION: (2,4)
PLAN: W W N N E

So I can pick up and deliver parcel 1 by t=17. Let me plan 5 moves from t=10 to get closer:

Let me recalculate: t=23 mod 8 = 7, so P is at (1,2). So (1,3) should be safe at t=23.

Actually, let me be more careful. At t=22, I'd be at (1,4). At t=23, I move N to (1,3). At t=23, t mod 8 = 7, so P is at (1,2). So (1,3) is safe.

Then at t=24, I move S to (1,4) to deliver parcel 1. At t=24, t mod 8 = 0, so P is at (2,2). Safe.

So I can pick up and deliver parcel 1 by t=24. Let me plan 5 moves from t=15:
